title: Creating a 3D Layered Mandala: Part 1 - Sketching in Photoshop
description:
Embark on a creative journey with us in this first installment of a six-part series, where we will craft a 3D Layered Mandala from scratch. 

Witness the complete transformation, from initial sketch to vector, from presentation to final cut. In this episode, we delve into the art of sketching a mandala, using Adobe Photoshop and the versatile Wacom Intuos 5 Pro Tablet. 

Enjoy an in-depth look into our process and pick up some tricks to create your own mandala design.

Don't forget to hit the like button if you find the video helpful and subscribe to stay tuned for the upcoming parts. 

Want to bring our mandala design to life? You can purchase the final SVG cut files from our shop: https://designbundles.net/pixaroma/2654600-decorative-mandala-3d-layered-svg-mandala-decor-wa

Your support is greatly appreciated!

Playlist with entire series here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NWzZCUjsv9I&list=PL-pohOSaL8P_yzx3y9QchBb1chfzFXObV
transcript: hello fellow designers and artists. This video is one of a series where I demonstrate the process  of creating a 3D layered mandala SVG cut file. In this initial video, I'll guide you through  sketching the mandala using Adobe Photoshop. While I personally use awaycom individuals and  pro tablet, you can follow along with an iPad or Android tablet and a stylus. Just make sure to  have an app that offers a mandala mirroring function. For this project, I've set the mandala  symmetry to a 4-segment count.  For the first layer, I'm creating a frame-like design to resemble a shadow box.  When sketching the 3D layered mandala, I begin by working on the uppermost layers and then  progress to the layers underneath. Throughout the sketching process, I keep in mind that the design  should form a single cohesive shape. This way, when the mandala is cut, it won't have numerous  individual pieces, but rather a single piece, making assembly much simpler.  Once the sketch is complete, I save it as a GPG file. In the next video, I will demonstrate  how I utilize the sketch to create a 3D layered vector file. Thank you for watching,  and if you found this video helpful, please remember to like and subscribe for more content in the  future.
